Enabled.Name: Enable Counterspell workflow
Enabled.Hint: Intercept recognized Counterspell spells and use the Counterspell PLUS workflow.

Ruleset.Name: Counterspell rules
Ruleset.Hint: Choose the rule used to resolve Counterspell.

ScrollDefenseBase.Name: Spell scroll defense base
ScrollDefenseBase.Hint: Base added to the stored spell level and creator values for a scroll. Default: 7. Any numeric value is allowed.

GlyphDefenseBase.Name: Spell Glyph defense base
GlyphDefenseBase.Hint: Base added to the stored spell level and creator values for a Glyph. Default: 10. Any numeric value is allowed.

Names.Name: Recognized Counterspell names
Names.Hint: Comma-separated spell names. Parenthetical suffixes such as Counterspell (Alternate) are recognized automatically.

WildMagic.Name: Wild Magic message
WildMagic.Hint: Post a Wild Magic message when Counterspell targets another Counterspell.

Dialog.KnowsTargetSpell: I know the spell being countered
Dialog.KnowsTargetSpellHint: Homebrew only: this grants advantage unless the target spell is Counterspell. The GM will confirm this declaration.
Dialog.GMConfirmKnowledge: The Counterspell caster knows the target spell
Dialog.GMConfirmKnowledgeHint: Confirm or correct the player's declaration. Knowing the spell never grants advantage against another Counterspell.
Dialog.DisadvantageHint: For example, from an exhaustion level or another effect. The GM will confirm this declaration.
Dialog.GMTargetDisadvantageHint: For example, from exhaustion or another effect. This option is ignored for a spell scroll or glyph.
Dialog.GMDisadvantageHint: Confirm or correct the declaration. Advantage and disadvantage cancel each other.
Dialog.GMWillReview: The active GM will receive and review all selected values before the rolls.
Dialog.TargetPlayerTitle: Counterspell PLUS — your spell is being countered
Dialog.AskOwner: Ask the active owner of this actor
Dialog.AskOwnerHint: If an active player owns the selected actor, they will receive their own data window.
Dialog.ScrollHint: Creator values are used only when Casting source is Spell scroll or Glyph.
Dialog.PlayerDataHint: Your modifier and proficiency bonus will be read from this character sheet. The GM will review the result.
Dialog.GMReviewHint: Confirm or correct the target spell data. The Counterspell slot is consumed only after you press Roll.

Chat.CounterspellRollAdvantage: Counterspell: {actor} — advantage for knowing the target spell
Chat.AutomaticSuccess: The Counterspell slot is at least as high as the target spell. The spell is countered automatically.
Chat.HiddenResults: At least one side used a hidden roll. The outcome is public, but the hidden numerical results remain visible only according to their selected roll modes.
Chat.TieRule: In the opposed-roll rule, a tie favors the original spell.
Chat.WildMagicBody: Counterspelling another Counterspell triggers a Wild Magic effect. The GM determines the effect.

Notifications.NoSlots: This actor has no available spell slot capable of casting Counterspell.
Notifications.NoCasters: No actors or tokens are available for the GM to select.
Notifications.CasterNotFound: The selected caster could not be found.
Notifications.ActorNotFound: The Counterspell actor could not be found.
Notifications.SlotUnavailable: The selected Counterspell slot is no longer available.
Notifications.NoGM: An active GM is required to resolve Counterspell.
Notifications.AlreadyPending: This actor already has a pending Counterspell workflow.
Notifications.RequestTimeout: The Counterspell request expired after three minutes.
Notifications.CancelledByGM: The GM cancelled the Counterspell workflow.
Notifications.CancelledByTarget: The target player cancelled the Counterspell workflow.
Notifications.TargetRollFailed: The target spell roll could not be completed.
Notifications.GenericError: The Counterspell workflow encountered an error.
